cases.title: Events list page 2 returns correct slice with total and totalPages per testing-plan §3
preconditions: At least 13 published events visible to the requesting actor (more than default pageSize of 12)
Authenticated participant or public access per GET /events visibility rules
steps: GET /api/v1/events?page=1&pageSize=12&sort=startAt:asc and record total and totalPages
GET /api/v1/events?page=2&pageSize=12&sort=startAt:asc
Compare page-2 items to the expected second slice ordered by startAt ascending
cases.expected: HTTP 200; page-2 response has page=2, pageSize=12, total matching page-1 total, totalPages=ceil(total/12), items.length <= 12, and items are the correct contiguous slice per testing-plan §3 row: Events list page 2 → correct slice + total (AC-13)

cases.title: Registration repository paginated query returns correct total and page-2 slice at DB boundary
preconditions: Event with exactly 25 registrations persisted in Postgres
Real database test harness with transaction rollback per test
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin actor with event scope
steps: Invoke registration list query via registration module/service with page=1, pageSize=20
Invoke same query with page=2, pageSize=20
Compare returned items to direct COUNT and OFFSET/LIMIT expectations in persistence layer
cases.expected: Page 1 returns 20 items with total=25 and totalPages=2; page 2 returns 5 items with same total metadata; module-integration boundary exercises real DB transaction without loading full unbounded dataset

cases.title: My registrations list returns paginated envelope with correct page-2 boundaries
preconditions: Participant with more than 20 registrations across events (or pageSize boundary fixture)
Authenticated participant JWT
steps: GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20
GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=2&pageSize=20
Verify each item includes registrationId, eventId, eventName, state, and updatedAt
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with paginated envelope; page 1 items.length=20; page 2 contains remaining rows; total and totalPages consistent across pages; default sort updatedAt:desc applied; items scoped to current actor only

cases.title: Audit logs list returns ordered paginated rows with total count per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Event with multiple audit log entries from critical config changes
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin with event scope
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs?page=1&pageSize=20
Verify ordering and envelope metadata against persistence row count
cases.expected: HTTP 200; items ordered by createdAt descending; total equals full matching audit row count; totalPages computed correctly per testing-plan §3 row: Audit logs paginated → ordered + total count (AC-13)

cases.title: GET /api/v1/events returns HTTP 200 paginated envelope with required fields
preconditions: At least one discoverable event seeded
Authenticated participant JWT or public access per endpoint rules
steps: GET /api/v1/events?page=1&pageSize=12 with Authorization bearer when required
Verify HTTP status code and response body shape
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items (array), page=1, pageSize=12, total (integer), and totalPages per API design §3 response envelope; no bare array response

cases.title: All eight paginated list endpoints share consistent envelope shape over HTTP
preconditions: Seeded data covering events, registrations, waitlist, attendance, eligibility, audit-logs, status-history, and me/registrations
Valid JWT tokens for participant and organizer actors with required scopes
steps: Issue paginated GET requests to each documented list endpoint with page=1 and endpoint default pageSize
Assert each response body contains items (array), page, pageSize, total, and totalPages
cases.expected: All eight paginated endpoints return the same envelope structure defined in API design section 3; HTTP 200 for authorized requests; no bare array responses

cases.title: Participant event browse paginated card grid shows range label (e.g. 1–12 of N)
preconditions: At least 13 discoverable events seeded (exceeds default pageSize of 12)
Authenticated participant logged in
Participant event discovery browse page reachable
steps: Navigate to participant event browse list page
Observe pagination range label or equivalent summary text
Navigate to page 2 using pagination controls
Observe updated range label reflecting second page slice
cases.expected: Page 1 displays range label such as Showing 1–12 of N where N is total matching events; page 2 displays Showing 13–24 of N (or equivalent upper bound); prev/next controls disabled at bounds; UI does not render full unbounded dataset client-side per listing pages §3 and page list event discovery

cases.title: Organizer registrations paginated table shows range label (e.g. 1–20 of N)
preconditions: Event with more than 20 registrations
OrganizerAdmin logged in with access to event registrations page at /organizer/events/{eventId}/registrations
steps: Navigate to organizer event registrations page
Observe pagination range label in table footer or toolbar
Navigate to page 2
Verify range label updates to reflect second page boundaries
cases.expected: Page 1 shows range label such as Showing 1–20 of N; page 2 shows Showing 21–40 of N (or equivalent); table renders only current page rows; prev/next pagination controls present per listing pages §3 and page list organizer registration management

cases.title: Participant denied paginated GET audit-logs with HTTP 403
preconditions: Event with audit log entries
Authenticated Participant JWT without audit log read permission per 01-roles-permissions.md
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/audit-logs?page=1&pageSize=20 with Participant Authorization bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 403 unauthorized; Participant role cannot read audit logs per permission matrix; paginated endpoint enforces RBAC before returning envelope

cases.title: OrganizerStaff denied paginated registrations list for unassigned event with HTTP 403
preconditions: Event outside OrganizerStaff assigned scope with registrations
Authenticated OrganizerStaff JWT for a different assigned event set
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{unassignedEventId}/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20 with OrganizerStaff Authorization bearer token
cases.expected: HTTP 403 unauthorized; OrganizerStaff scope constraint blocks paginated list access for events outside assigned scope per 01-roles-permissions.md §3

cases.title: Invalid pagination parameters return HTTP 400 INVALID_PAGINATION
preconditions: Any paginated list endpoint accessible to authenticated actor
Valid JWT where endpoint requires auth
steps: GET with page=0&pageSize=20
GET with page=1&pageSize=101
GET with page=abc&pageSize=20
cases.expected: Each invalid request returns HTTP 400 with error.code INVALID_PAGINATION for page < 1, pageSize above max (100), or non-integer values per API design §3 validation rules

cases.title: Page beyond totalPages returns empty items with valid metadata
preconditions: Paginated list endpoint with known total row count (e.g. GET /events with total <= 12)
Valid authentication where required
steps: GET list endpoint with page=1 and pageSize=20; record total and totalPages
GET same endpoint with page=totalPages+1 and same pageSize
cases.expected: HTTP 200; beyond-last-page response has items: [], page equals requested page, pageSize unchanged, total and totalPages match page-1 metadata per AC-13 and API design section 3

cases.title: Registrations list never returns more than pageSize items per testing-plan §3
preconditions: Event with total registrations exceeding configured pageSize (e.g. 25 rows, pageSize=20)
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin with event scope
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?page=2&pageSize=20
cases.expected: Page 1 items.length=20; page 2 items.length=5; neither page exceeds pageSize; total=25 and totalPages=2 per testing-plan §3 row: Registrations list beyond capacity → never returns more than pageSize items (AC-13, NFR-16)

cases.title: Waitlist module paginated query preserves FIFO position order across pages
preconditions: Full event with waitlist enabled and more than 20 Waitlisted participants (exceeds default pageSize)
Real Postgres test database
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin with event scope
steps: Invoke waitlist list query via registration/waitlist module with page=1, pageSize=20
Invoke same query with page=2, pageSize=20
Verify items ordering and total against persistence layer across both pages
cases.expected: Paginated envelope with items sorted by position ascending on each page; page 1 positions 1–20 and page 2 continues FIFO without gaps or reordering; total reflects full waitlist queue length; module-integration exercises DB boundary for GET /events/{eventId}/waitlist per AC-02f

cases.title: Attendance list returns paginated check-in rows with checkinAt descending sort
preconditions: Event with multiple recorded check-ins
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin or OrganizerStaff with event scope
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/attendance?page=1&pageSize=20
GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/attendance?page=2&pageSize=20 when total exceeds 20
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with paginated envelope; items sorted by checkinAt descending; total matches attendance row count; page boundaries correct when multiple pages exist

cases.title: totalPages uses ceiling division when total is not divisible by pageSize
preconditions: List endpoint with exactly total=25 rows available
pageSize=20
steps: Request page=1 with pageSize=20 and read totalPages
Request page=2 and verify non-empty items
cases.expected: totalPages=2 (ceil(25/20)); page 2 contains remaining 5 items; metadata consistent across both pages

cases.title: Empty matching result set returns total zero with valid envelope
preconditions: Event with no registrations (or empty result set after filters)
Authenticated OrganizerAdmin with event scope
steps: GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with items: [], total=0, totalPages=1 (or consistent zero-total convention), page=1, pageSize=20

cases.title: Omitted page and pageSize use endpoint defaults on GET /events
preconditions: GET /events accessible with sufficient seeded events
Valid auth if required
steps: GET /api/v1/events without page or pageSize query parameters
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with page=1, pageSize=12 (events endpoint default), and correctly paginated first slice

cases.title: Paginated list returns single page within acceptable time when total exceeds 500 rows
preconditions: Database seeded with >500 rows for a paginated endpoint (e.g. audit-logs or registrations)
OrganizerAdmin actor with event scope
Test environment uses fixed clock and deterministic fixtures per testing plan section 7
steps: Record start time
GET paginated list with page=1 and pageSize=20
Record elapsed response time
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with exactly one page of items (length <= pageSize); response completes within acceptable latency threshold per NFR-16; total reflects full matching row count without loading all rows into the response body

cases.title: total and totalPages remain stable across sequential page requests
preconditions: List endpoint with at least three pages of data at pageSize=10
Authenticated actor with list access
steps: GET page=1, page=2, and page=3 with pageSize=10
Compare total and totalPages on each response
cases.expected: total and totalPages are identical on every page; only items array differs between pages; supports reliable client-side pagination controls

cases.title: Organizer events paginated table shows range label (e.g. Showing 1–20 of N)
preconditions: At least 21 organizer-visible events seeded (exceeds organizer events table pageSize of 20)
OrganizerAdmin logged in with access to /organizer/events
steps: Navigate to organizer event management list page
Observe pagination range label in table footer or toolbar
Navigate to page 2 using pagination controls
Verify range label updates to reflect second page boundaries
cases.expected: Page 1 shows range label such as Showing 1–20 of N; page 2 shows Showing 21–40 of N (or equivalent); table renders only current page rows; server-driven pagination per listing pages §3 and page list organizer events table
